title: Elwendia kopetdagensis
taxonomy: Elwendia kopetdagensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It is native to the Kopet Dagh Mountains in Turkmenistan. The classification of Elwendia kopetdagensis includes its placement in the genus Elwendia, which is a part of the Ranunculaceae family. Further classification involves its order Ranunculales, class Magnoliopsida, and division Magnoliophyta. Elwendia kopetdagensis is also known to be a member of the Angiosperm Phylogeny Group's classification system.

intro: Elwendia kopetdagensis is a perennial plant species belonging to the Apiaceae family, native to Central Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in treating various ailments, including digestive issues and infections. The bioactive constituents of Elwendia kopetdagensis include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Elwendia kopetdagensis are infusions, decoctions, and extracts, often used to treat digestive disorders and respiratory issues.
